content.text:
I now have a comprehensive understanding of the entire upload pipeline. Let me compile the analysis.

## Analysis: CLI Upload Functionality

### Overview

The CLI provides three distinct upload paths: (1) an interactive multi-project batch upload via `rudel upload` (no arguments), (2) a single-session upload via `rudel upload <session>`, and (3) an automatic hook-based upload triggered by Claude Code's `SessionEnd` event. The `rudel enable` command installs this hook and optionally bulk-uploads existing sessions. All paths converge on a shared `uploadSession()` function that sends an oRPC call to the server's `ingestSession` endpoint.

### Entry Points

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts:267-275` -- `runUpload()` dispatches to either interactive or single mode
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts:15` -- `runEnable()` for the enable command
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/hook-upload.ts:19` -- `runHookUpload()` for the legacy hook path
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts:29` -- `runSessionEnd()` for the new hook path
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/app.ts:13-29` -- route map wiring (`hooks` and `dev` are hidden from help)

### Core Implementation

---

#### 1. The `upload` Command -- Two Modes

**Dispatch logic** (`upload.ts:267-275`): If no positional arguments are provided, `runInteractiveUpload()` is called. If a session argument is provided, `runSingleUpload()` is called with `sessions[0]`.

##### 1a. Interactive Mode (`upload.ts:33-145`)

**Authentication check** (`upload.ts:34-39`): Calls `loadCredentials()` to read `~/.rudel/credentials.json`. If no credentials exist and `--dry-run` is not set, prints an error and sets `process.exitCode = 1`.

**Project scanning** (`upload.ts:44-46`): Calls `scanProjects()` which reads all directories under `~/.claude/projects/`, filters `.jsonl` files (excluding `agent-*` files), and returns `ScannedProject[]` objects containing decoded paths, session IDs, and session counts.

**Grouping by git remote** (`upload.ts:55-57`): Calls `groupProjectsByRemote(projects, cwd)` which:
- Resolves git remote URLs for all projects in parallel (`project-scanner.ts:117-118`)
- Groups projects sharing the same normalized remote URL into `ProjectGroup` objects (`project-scanner.ts:121-158`)
- Uses a file-based cache at `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json` to look up remotes for projects whose directories no longer exist (`project-scanner.ts:146-154`, `remote-cache.ts:1-42`)
- Runs a second pass that matches ungrouped projects (no git remote, no cache hit) to existing groups by filesystem path similarity, requiring the common prefix to extend beyond the home directory and to be strictly better than any second-best match (`project-scanner.ts:178-195`, `project-scanner.ts:261-295`)
- Sorts groups: cwd-containing groups first, then groups with remotes, then alphabetically (`project-scanner.ts:209-215`)

**User selection** (`upload.ts:59-91`): Presents a `@clack/prompts` multi-select. Options display either a single path or the group's display name (owner/repo). Groups containing the current working directory are pre-selected (`upload.ts:76-78`).

**Batch upload** (`upload.ts:99-119`): Flattens selected groups into individual projects, constructs a `BatchOptions` object, and calls `batchUpload()`.

**Progress reporting** (`upload.ts:110-118`): A `@clack/prompts` spinner is started before the batch. A callback passed to `batchUpload()` updates the spinner message with `[current/total] Uploading...` on each session.

**Result reporting** (`upload.ts:121-144`): After the batch completes, reports succeeded/failed counts. If failures occurred, prints up to 5 error details with `project/sessionId: error` format (`upload.ts:128-133`), and indicates how many additional errors were truncated. Sets `process.exitCode = 1` if any failures.

##### 1b. Single Session Mode (`upload.ts:151-265`)

**Session resolution** (`upload.ts:170-179`): Calls `resolveSession(session)` which determines whether the input is a path (contains `/` or ends with `.jsonl`) or a session ID.

- **Path resolution** (`session-resolver.ts:27-45`): Validates the file exists via `Bun.file()`, extracts the session ID from the filename, and decodes the parent directory name back to a filesystem path.
- **ID resolution** (`session-resolver.ts:47-76`): Iterates all directories under `~/.claude/projects/`, reads each directory's file list, and returns the first match.
- **Subagent validation** (`session-resolver.ts:78-84`): Both paths call `validateNotSubagent()` which throws if the filename starts with `agent-`.
- **Path decoding** (`session-resolver.ts:92-138`): `decodeProjectPath()` takes an encoded directory name (dashes replacing slashes, e.g. `-Users-marc-Workspace-foo`) and uses a recursive backtracking algorithm. It splits on `-`, then tries progressively longer dash-joined segments, calling `stat()` to check if each candidate path actually exists on the filesystem. Falls back to simple dash-to-slash replacement if no existing path is found.

**Transcript reading** (`upload.ts:183-193`): Calls `readTranscript()` which has a retry loop.

**Subagent extraction** (`upload.ts:195-207`): Calls `extractAgentIds(content)` to parse every JSON line, collecting `toolUseResult.agentId` values into a deduplicated set (`transcript-reader.ts:37-54`). Then calls `readSubagentFiles()` which checks two possible locations for each agent ID:
1. `{sessionDir}/agent-{agentId}.jsonl` (legacy format)
2. `{sessionDir}/{sessionId}/subagents/agent-{agentId}.jsonl` (new format)

The function tries each path in order and takes the first that exists (`subagent-reader.ts:18-36`).

**Git info** (`upload.ts:209-211`): Calls `getGitInfo(projectPath)` which runs four git/filesystem operations in parallel (`git-info.ts:26-31`):
1. `git remote get-url origin` for the remote URL
2. `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` for the branch
3. `git rev-parse HEAD` for the SHA
4. Reads `package.json` from the git root for the package name

The `repository` field is derived from the normalized git remote (owner/repo), falling back to `packageName`, then to the directory name (`git-info.ts:49-65`).

**Classification** (`upload.ts:213-218`): If `--classify` is set and no `--tag` was given, calls `classifySession(content)`. This truncates the content to 50,000 bytes, writes it to a temp file, and shells out to the `claude` CLI with `--model haiku --print --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions` (`classifier.ts:41-48`). Parses the output for an exact match or regex word-boundary match against the known tags. Falls back to `"other"` on non-zero exit or no match; returns `undefined` on exception (`classifier.ts:53-72`).

**Organization resolution** (`upload.ts:220-221`): Uses `--org` flag if provided, otherwise calls `getProjectOrgId(projectPath)` which looks up the project in `~/.rudel/projects.json` keyed by normalized git remote URL (or absolute path fallback) (`project-config.ts:39-73`).

**Request assembly** (`upload.ts:224-236`): Constructs an `IngestRequest` with: `sessionId`, `projectPath`, `repository`, `gitRemote`, `packageName`, `gitBranch`, `gitSha`, `tag`, `content`, `subagents`, `organizationId`.

**Dry run** (`upload.ts:238-250`): If `--dry-run`, replaces content/subagent content with byte-count placeholders and prints the JSON.

**Upload** (`upload.ts:253-264`): Calls `uploadSession(request, config)`.

---

#### 2. The Batch Uploader (`batch-uploader.ts`)

**`uploadOneSession()`** (`batch-uploader.ts:24-80`): Handles a single session within a batch. Reads the transcript, extracts subagents, resolves git info, optionally classifies, resolves org ID, assembles the `IngestRequest`, and calls `uploadSession()`. Returns `{ success: boolean, error?: string }`.

**`batchUpload()`** (`batch-uploader.ts:82-120`): Iterates projects and their session IDs **sequentially** (nested `for` loops at lines 94-95). For each session:
1. Increments a `completed` counter and calls the `onProgress` callback
2. Calls `uploadOneSession()` with the session's directory, ID, and decoded path
3. Accumulates results into a `BatchResult` with `succeeded`, `failed`, and `errors` arrays

There is no concurrency or parallelism -- sessions upload one at a time.

---

#### 3. The Upload Function (`uploader.ts`)

**`uploadSession()`** (`uploader.ts:15-34`): Creates an oRPC client with `RPCLink` pointed at the configured endpoint URL, sets the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, and calls `client.ingestSession(request)`. On success, returns `{ success: true, status: 200 }`. On any error (network, server, etc.), catches and returns `{ success: false, error: String(error) }`.

There is **no retry logic** in the upload function itself. If the oRPC call fails, the error is returned immediately.

---

#### 4. The `enable` Command (`enable.ts`)

**Authentication** (`enable.ts:19-25`): Calls `verifyAuth()` which loads credentials and then makes an actual API call (`client.me()`) to validate the token is still valid (`auth.ts:22-60`). If the token is expired/invalid (401/403), `verifyAuth()` clears the stored credentials file and returns a `token_expired` failure.

**Organization selection** (`enable.ts:30-83`): Calls `client.listMyOrganizations()` via the oRPC API client (`api-client.ts:11-21`). If only one org exists, auto-selects it. If multiple orgs and one matches the existing config, uses that. Otherwise presents a `@clack/prompts` select prompt.

**Persist org ID** (`enable.ts:85`): Calls `setProjectOrgId(cwd, selectedOrgId)` which writes to `~/.rudel/projects.json`.

**Hook installation** (`enable.ts:88-95`): Gets a `ClaudeCodeAgent` instance (`agents/index.ts:7-8`), checks if the hook is already installed, and if not, calls `agent.installHook()`.

The hook installation (`claude-settings.ts:77-97`) reads `{.claude-dir}/settings.json`, checks if a `SessionEnd` hook with the command `"rudel hooks claude session-end"` already exists, and if not, appends a new entry:
```json
{
  "matcher": "",
  "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "rudel hooks claude session-end", "async": true }]
}
```

The `.claude` directory is found by walking up from cwd looking for an existing `.claude` directory, falling back to git root, then cwd (`claude-settings.ts:26-48`).

**Bulk upload of existing sessions** (`enable.ts:97-168`):
1. Calls `findSessionsForCwd(cwd)` (`project-scanner.ts:239-248`) which scans all projects, groups by remote, finds the group containing cwd, and returns all `SessionFile` objects from that group.
2. If sessions exist, prompts the user with a confirm dialog (default: `false`) (`enable.ts:104-107`).
3. If confirmed, uses `p.tasks()` to process each session sequentially. Each task:
   - Reads the transcript
   - Extracts agent IDs and reads subagent files
   - Resolves git info
   - Constructs an `IngestRequest` (with the selected `organizationId`)
   - Calls `uploadSession()` directly
   - Returns a status string ("Uploaded", "Failed: ...", or "Error: ...")
   - Increments `failed` counter on errors (`enable.ts:117-162`)

The `enable` command does **not** use `batchUpload()` -- it calls `uploadSession()` directly inside `p.tasks()`.

---

#### 5. Hook-Based Automatic Upload (Two Implementations)

There are two hook upload implementations. The installed hook runs `rudel hooks claude session-end`.

##### 5a. `hooks claude session-end` (`hooks/claude/session-end.ts:29-85`)

This is the path actually invoked by the installed Claude Code hook.

1. Sets up structured file logging to `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` (`logging.ts:9-24`)
2. Reads stdin (the hook payload from Claude Code) and parses it as `HookInput` (`session-end.ts:21-27, 34-38`)
3. Loads credentials from `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`session-end.ts:40-41`). Silently returns if not logged in.
4. Reads the transcript with retry logic
5. Extracts subagent IDs and reads subagent files
6. Resolves git info from the working directory
7. Resolves the organization ID from `~/.rudel/projects.json` (`session-end.ts:58`)
8. Constructs the `IngestRequest` including `gitRemote` and `packageName` fields
9. Uses `RUDEL_API_BASE` env var or falls back to `credentials.apiBaseUrl` for the endpoint (`session-end.ts:73-74`)
10. Calls `uploadSession()` and logs success/failure
11. Entire function is wrapped in try/catch; all errors are caught and logged silently (`session-end.ts:80-81`)

##### 5b. `hook-upload` command (`hook-upload.ts:19-104`)

This appears to be a legacy/alternative hook path. It:
1. Reads from a `GazedConfig` at `~/.config/gazed/config.json` instead of `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`config.ts:5-6`, `hook-upload.ts:26-31`)
2. Uses the `readStdin()` and `parseStdinInput()` helpers from `stdin.ts` which handle TTY detection and Windows compatibility (`stdin.ts:14-40`)
3. Runs classification in parallel with git info resolution (`hook-upload.ts:56-59`)
4. Uses `GAZED_INGEST_ENDPOINT` env var, then `config.endpoint`, then `DEFAULT_ENDPOINT` (`hook-upload.ts:72-73`)
5. Does **not** resolve organization ID or include `gitRemote`/`packageName`

---

#### 6. Transcript Reading and Retry Logic (`transcript-reader.ts`)

**`readTranscript()`** (`transcript-reader.ts:4-31`): The **only** retry logic in the entire upload pipeline. It retries up to 5 times with 500ms delays. On each attempt:
- Checks if the file exists via `Bun.file().exists()`
- If the file doesn't exist, waits and retries (unless it's the last attempt, in which case throws)
- If reading throws any error, waits and retries (unless last attempt)

This retry is designed for timing issues where the transcript file may not be fully written yet (relevant for hook-triggered uploads where Claude Code is still finishing up).

---

### Data Flow

#### Interactive Upload Flow
1. `runUpload()` at `upload.ts:267` -- dispatches to interactive mode (no args)
2. `loadCredentials()` at `credentials.ts:35` -- reads `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
3. `scanProjects()` at `project-scanner.ts:28` -- reads `~/.claude/projects/` directories
4. `groupProjectsByRemote()` at `project-scanner.ts:110` -- groups by git remote with caching
5. User selects projects via `@clack/prompts` multiselect
6. `batchUpload()` at `batch-uploader.ts:82` -- sequential iteration
7. For each session: `uploadOneSession()` at `batch-uploader.ts:24`
   - `readTranscript()` at `transcript-reader.ts:4` (with 5x retry)
   - `extractAgentIds()` at `transcript-reader.ts:37`
   - `readSubagentFiles()` at `subagent-reader.ts:10`
   - `getGitInfo()` at `git-info.ts:25`
   - Optionally `classifySession()` at `classifier.ts:21`
   - `getProjectOrgId()` at `project-config.ts:68`
   - `uploadSession()` at `uploader.ts:15` -- oRPC call to server

#### Enable Flow
1. `runEnable()` at `enable.ts:15`
2. `verifyAuth()` at `auth.ts:22` -- loads credentials + API ping
3. `client.listMyOrganizations()` via `api-client.ts:11`
4. User selects org (or auto-select if single)
5. `setProjectOrgId()` at `project-config.ts:76` -- persists to `~/.rudel/projects.json`
6. `agent.installHook()` at `claude-code.ts:22` --> `addHook()` at `claude-settings.ts:77` -- writes to `.claude/settings.json`
7. `findSessionsForCwd()` at `project-scanner.ts:239` -- scans + groups + finds cwd group
8. User confirms bulk upload
9. `p.tasks()` iterates each session: read transcript, resolve git, upload

#### Hook Flow (Automatic)
1. Claude Code session ends, triggers `rudel hooks claude session-end`
2. `runSessionEnd()` at `session-end.ts:29`
3. Reads JSON from stdin (session_id, transcript_path, cwd)
4. `loadCredentials()` -- checks auth
5. `readTranscript()` -- with 5x retry
6. `extractAgentIds()` + `readSubagentFiles()`
7. `getGitInfo(cwd)` + `getProjectOrgId(cwd)`
8. `uploadSession()` -- single oRPC call

---

### Key Patterns

- **Agent Abstraction**: `CodingAgent` interface at `agents/types.ts:7-15` allows plugging in different coding tools. Currently only `ClaudeCodeAgent` is implemented (`agents/claude-code.ts:19`). `getDefaultAgent()` at `agents/index.ts:7` hardcodes `ClaudeCodeAgent`.
- **Remote Cache**: `remote-cache.ts` persists a `Record<string, string>` mapping encoded directory names to normalized git remote URLs at `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json`. Written fire-and-forget after grouping (`project-scanner.ts:218-220`).
- **Path Encoding/Decoding**: Claude Code encodes project paths by replacing `/` with `-`. Decoding uses recursive backtracking with filesystem `stat()` calls to handle ambiguous segments containing dashes (`session-resolver.ts:92-138`).
- **Two Credential Systems**: The newer path uses `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`credentials.ts`), while `hook-upload.ts` reads a legacy config from `~/.config/gazed/config.json` (`config.ts:5-6`).

### Error Handling

| Location | Error Type | Handling |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `upload.ts:34-39` | Missing credentials | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:171-179` | Session resolution failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:184-192` | Transcript read failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:259-264` | Upload failure | Prints error from `UploadResult`, sets `process.exitCode = 1` |
| `batch-uploader.ts:33-39` | Transcript read failure in batch | Returns `{ success: false, error }`, counted in `result.failed` |
| `batch-uploader.ts:75-79` | Upload failure in batch | Returns `{ success: false, error }`, counted in `result.failed` |
| `uploader.ts:31-33` | Any oRPC error | Caught, returns `{ success: false, error: String(error) }` |
| `enable.ts:33-38` | Org fetch failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1` |
| `enable.ts:156-159` | Session upload error in enable | Increments `failed` counter, returns error string as task result |
| `session-end.ts:80-81` | Any error in hook | Logged to file, never shown to user |
| `auth.ts:36-59` | Token verification | 401/403 clears stored credentials; other errors return `network_error` |

### Retry Logic

The **only** retry logic exists in `readTranscript()` at `transcript-reader.ts:4-31`:
- **Max retries**: 5
- **Delay**: 500ms between attempts
- **Scope**: File existence check and file read
- **Purpose**: Handle timing issues where transcript file is not yet fully written

There is **no retry logic** for:
- The oRPC upload call (`uploader.ts`)
- Git info resolution (`git-info.ts`)
- Subagent file reading (`subagent-reader.ts`)
- Classification (`classifier.ts`)

### Progress Reporting

| Context | Mechanism | Details |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Interactive upload | `@clack/prompts` spinner | Shows `[current/total] Uploading...` via callback at `upload.ts:116-118` |
| Interactive upload | Post-batch summary | Success count at `upload.ts:123-125`, failure count + first 5 errors at `upload.ts:126-134` |
| Single upload | `process.stdout.write` | Step-by-step text output (resolving, reading, transcript size, repo, branch, uploading, result) |
| Enable bulk upload | `p.tasks()` | Each task shows title `[i/total] sessionId`, updates message through "Reading transcript...", "Resolving git info...", "Uploading..." at `enable.ts:117-161` |
| Hook upload | File logging only | All output goes to `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` via `@logtape/logtape` at `logging.ts:7` |

### Configuration Files

| File | Purpose | Written by |
|------|---------|------------|
| `~/.rudel/credentials.json` | Auth token + API base URL | `credentials.ts:24-33` via `saveCredentials()` |
| `~/.rudel/projects.json` | Project-to-org mapping (keyed by normalized git remote or path) | `project-config.ts:29-37` via `setProjectOrgId()` |
| `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json` | Encoded-dir-to-remote mapping | `remote-cache.ts:33-42` via `cacheRemotes()` |
| `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` | Hook upload logs | `logging.ts:7` |
| `~/.config/gazed/config.json` | Legacy API key + endpoint config | `config.ts:5` (read by `hook-upload.ts`) |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Claude Code hook configuration | `claude-settings.ts:62-66` via `writeClaudeSettings()` |

### Command Flags (`upload`)

Defined at `upload.ts:277-329`:

| Flag | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `--tag` / `-t` | Enum (SESSION_TAGS) | None | Manual session tag |
| `--endpoint` | String | `https://app.rudel.ai/rpc` | Override upload URL |
| `--classify` / `-c` | Boolean | `false` | Auto-classify via Claude CLI |
| `--dry-run` / `-n` | Boolean | `false` | Preview without uploading |
| `--org` / `-o` | String | None | Override organization ID |

I now have a comprehensive understanding of the entire upload pipeline. Let me compile the analysis.

## Analysis: CLI Upload Functionality

### Overview

The CLI provides three distinct upload paths: (1) an interactive multi-project batch upload via `rudel upload` (no arguments), (2) a single-session upload via `rudel upload <session>`, and (3) an automatic hook-based upload triggered by Claude Code's `SessionEnd` event. The `rudel enable` command installs this hook and optionally bulk-uploads existing sessions. All paths converge on a shared `uploadSession()` function that sends an oRPC call to the server's `ingestSession` endpoint.

### Entry Points

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts:267-275` -- `runUpload()` dispatches to either interactive or single mode
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts:15` -- `runEnable()` for the enable command
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/hook-upload.ts:19` -- `runHookUpload()` for the legacy hook path
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts:29` -- `runSessionEnd()` for the new hook path
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/apps/cli/src/app.ts:13-29` -- route map wiring (`hooks` and `dev` are hidden from help)

### Core Implementation

---

#### 1. The `upload` Command -- Two Modes

**Dispatch logic** (`upload.ts:267-275`): If no positional arguments are provided, `runInteractiveUpload()` is called. If a session argument is provided, `runSingleUpload()` is called with `sessions[0]`.

##### 1a. Interactive Mode (`upload.ts:33-145`)

**Authentication check** (`upload.ts:34-39`): Calls `loadCredentials()` to read `~/.rudel/credentials.json`. If no credentials exist and `--dry-run` is not set, prints an error and sets `process.exitCode = 1`.

**Project scanning** (`upload.ts:44-46`): Calls `scanProjects()` which reads all directories under `~/.claude/projects/`, filters `.jsonl` files (excluding `agent-*` files), and returns `ScannedProject[]` objects containing decoded paths, session IDs, and session counts.

**Grouping by git remote** (`upload.ts:55-57`): Calls `groupProjectsByRemote(projects, cwd)` which:
- Resolves git remote URLs for all projects in parallel (`project-scanner.ts:117-118`)
- Groups projects sharing the same normalized remote URL into `ProjectGroup` objects (`project-scanner.ts:121-158`)
- Uses a file-based cache at `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json` to look up remotes for projects whose directories no longer exist (`project-scanner.ts:146-154`, `remote-cache.ts:1-42`)
- Runs a second pass that matches ungrouped projects (no git remote, no cache hit) to existing groups by filesystem path similarity, requiring the common prefix to extend beyond the home directory and to be strictly better than any second-best match (`project-scanner.ts:178-195`, `project-scanner.ts:261-295`)
- Sorts groups: cwd-containing groups first, then groups with remotes, then alphabetically (`project-scanner.ts:209-215`)

**User selection** (`upload.ts:59-91`): Presents a `@clack/prompts` multi-select. Options display either a single path or the group's display name (owner/repo). Groups containing the current working directory are pre-selected (`upload.ts:76-78`).

**Batch upload** (`upload.ts:99-119`): Flattens selected groups into individual projects, constructs a `BatchOptions` object, and calls `batchUpload()`.

**Progress reporting** (`upload.ts:110-118`): A `@clack/prompts` spinner is started before the batch. A callback passed to `batchUpload()` updates the spinner message with `[current/total] Uploading...` on each session.

**Result reporting** (`upload.ts:121-144`): After the batch completes, reports succeeded/failed counts. If failures occurred, prints up to 5 error details with `project/sessionId: error` format (`upload.ts:128-133`), and indicates how many additional errors were truncated. Sets `process.exitCode = 1` if any failures.

##### 1b. Single Session Mode (`upload.ts:151-265`)

**Session resolution** (`upload.ts:170-179`): Calls `resolveSession(session)` which determines whether the input is a path (contains `/` or ends with `.jsonl`) or a session ID.

- **Path resolution** (`session-resolver.ts:27-45`): Validates the file exists via `Bun.file()`, extracts the session ID from the filename, and decodes the parent directory name back to a filesystem path.
- **ID resolution** (`session-resolver.ts:47-76`): Iterates all directories under `~/.claude/projects/`, reads each directory's file list, and returns the first match.
- **Subagent validation** (`session-resolver.ts:78-84`): Both paths call `validateNotSubagent()` which throws if the filename starts with `agent-`.
- **Path decoding** (`session-resolver.ts:92-138`): `decodeProjectPath()` takes an encoded directory name (dashes replacing slashes, e.g. `-Users-marc-Workspace-foo`) and uses a recursive backtracking algorithm. It splits on `-`, then tries progressively longer dash-joined segments, calling `stat()` to check if each candidate path actually exists on the filesystem. Falls back to simple dash-to-slash replacement if no existing path is found.

**Transcript reading** (`upload.ts:183-193`): Calls `readTranscript()` which has a retry loop.

**Subagent extraction** (`upload.ts:195-207`): Calls `extractAgentIds(content)` to parse every JSON line, collecting `toolUseResult.agentId` values into a deduplicated set (`transcript-reader.ts:37-54`). Then calls `readSubagentFiles()` which checks two possible locations for each agent ID:
1. `{sessionDir}/agent-{agentId}.jsonl` (legacy format)
2. `{sessionDir}/{sessionId}/subagents/agent-{agentId}.jsonl` (new format)

The function tries each path in order and takes the first that exists (`subagent-reader.ts:18-36`).

**Git info** (`upload.ts:209-211`): Calls `getGitInfo(projectPath)` which runs four git/filesystem operations in parallel (`git-info.ts:26-31`):
1. `git remote get-url origin` for the remote URL
2. `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` for the branch
3. `git rev-parse HEAD` for the SHA
4. Reads `package.json` from the git root for the package name

The `repository` field is derived from the normalized git remote (owner/repo), falling back to `packageName`, then to the directory name (`git-info.ts:49-65`).

**Classification** (`upload.ts:213-218`): If `--classify` is set and no `--tag` was given, calls `classifySession(content)`. This truncates the content to 50,000 bytes, writes it to a temp file, and shells out to the `claude` CLI with `--model haiku --print --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions` (`classifier.ts:41-48`). Parses the output for an exact match or regex word-boundary match against the known tags. Falls back to `"other"` on non-zero exit or no match; returns `undefined` on exception (`classifier.ts:53-72`).

**Organization resolution** (`upload.ts:220-221`): Uses `--org` flag if provided, otherwise calls `getProjectOrgId(projectPath)` which looks up the project in `~/.rudel/projects.json` keyed by normalized git remote URL (or absolute path fallback) (`project-config.ts:39-73`).

**Request assembly** (`upload.ts:224-236`): Constructs an `IngestRequest` with: `sessionId`, `projectPath`, `repository`, `gitRemote`, `packageName`, `gitBranch`, `gitSha`, `tag`, `content`, `subagents`, `organizationId`.

**Dry run** (`upload.ts:238-250`): If `--dry-run`, replaces content/subagent content with byte-count placeholders and prints the JSON.

**Upload** (`upload.ts:253-264`): Calls `uploadSession(request, config)`.

---

#### 2. The Batch Uploader (`batch-uploader.ts`)

**`uploadOneSession()`** (`batch-uploader.ts:24-80`): Handles a single session within a batch. Reads the transcript, extracts subagents, resolves git info, optionally classifies, resolves org ID, assembles the `IngestRequest`, and calls `uploadSession()`. Returns `{ success: boolean, error?: string }`.

**`batchUpload()`** (`batch-uploader.ts:82-120`): Iterates projects and their session IDs **sequentially** (nested `for` loops at lines 94-95). For each session:
1. Increments a `completed` counter and calls the `onProgress` callback
2. Calls `uploadOneSession()` with the session's directory, ID, and decoded path
3. Accumulates results into a `BatchResult` with `succeeded`, `failed`, and `errors` arrays

There is no concurrency or parallelism -- sessions upload one at a time.

---

#### 3. The Upload Function (`uploader.ts`)

**`uploadSession()`** (`uploader.ts:15-34`): Creates an oRPC client with `RPCLink` pointed at the configured endpoint URL, sets the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, and calls `client.ingestSession(request)`. On success, returns `{ success: true, status: 200 }`. On any error (network, server, etc.), catches and returns `{ success: false, error: String(error) }`.

There is **no retry logic** in the upload function itself. If the oRPC call fails, the error is returned immediately.

---

#### 4. The `enable` Command (`enable.ts`)

**Authentication** (`enable.ts:19-25`): Calls `verifyAuth()` which loads credentials and then makes an actual API call (`client.me()`) to validate the token is still valid (`auth.ts:22-60`). If the token is expired/invalid (401/403), `verifyAuth()` clears the stored credentials file and returns a `token_expired` failure.

**Organization selection** (`enable.ts:30-83`): Calls `client.listMyOrganizations()` via the oRPC API client (`api-client.ts:11-21`). If only one org exists, auto-selects it. If multiple orgs and one matches the existing config, uses that. Otherwise presents a `@clack/prompts` select prompt.

**Persist org ID** (`enable.ts:85`): Calls `setProjectOrgId(cwd, selectedOrgId)` which writes to `~/.rudel/projects.json`.

**Hook installation** (`enable.ts:88-95`): Gets a `ClaudeCodeAgent` instance (`agents/index.ts:7-8`), checks if the hook is already installed, and if not, calls `agent.installHook()`.

The hook installation (`claude-settings.ts:77-97`) reads `{.claude-dir}/settings.json`, checks if a `SessionEnd` hook with the command `"rudel hooks claude session-end"` already exists, and if not, appends a new entry:
```json
{
  "matcher": "",
  "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "rudel hooks claude session-end", "async": true }]
}
```

The `.claude` directory is found by walking up from cwd looking for an existing `.claude` directory, falling back to git root, then cwd (`claude-settings.ts:26-48`).

**Bulk upload of existing sessions** (`enable.ts:97-168`):
1. Calls `findSessionsForCwd(cwd)` (`project-scanner.ts:239-248`) which scans all projects, groups by remote, finds the group containing cwd, and returns all `SessionFile` objects from that group.
2. If sessions exist, prompts the user with a confirm dialog (default: `false`) (`enable.ts:104-107`).
3. If confirmed, uses `p.tasks()` to process each session sequentially. Each task:
   - Reads the transcript
   - Extracts agent IDs and reads subagent files
   - Resolves git info
   - Constructs an `IngestRequest` (with the selected `organizationId`)
   - Calls `uploadSession()` directly
   - Returns a status string ("Uploaded", "Failed: ...", or "Error: ...")
   - Increments `failed` counter on errors (`enable.ts:117-162`)

The `enable` command does **not** use `batchUpload()` -- it calls `uploadSession()` directly inside `p.tasks()`.

---

#### 5. Hook-Based Automatic Upload (Two Implementations)

There are two hook upload implementations. The installed hook runs `rudel hooks claude session-end`.

##### 5a. `hooks claude session-end` (`hooks/claude/session-end.ts:29-85`)

This is the path actually invoked by the installed Claude Code hook.

1. Sets up structured file logging to `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` (`logging.ts:9-24`)
2. Reads stdin (the hook payload from Claude Code) and parses it as `HookInput` (`session-end.ts:21-27, 34-38`)
3. Loads credentials from `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`session-end.ts:40-41`). Silently returns if not logged in.
4. Reads the transcript with retry logic
5. Extracts subagent IDs and reads subagent files
6. Resolves git info from the working directory
7. Resolves the organization ID from `~/.rudel/projects.json` (`session-end.ts:58`)
8. Constructs the `IngestRequest` including `gitRemote` and `packageName` fields
9. Uses `RUDEL_API_BASE` env var or falls back to `credentials.apiBaseUrl` for the endpoint (`session-end.ts:73-74`)
10. Calls `uploadSession()` and logs success/failure
11. Entire function is wrapped in try/catch; all errors are caught and logged silently (`session-end.ts:80-81`)

##### 5b. `hook-upload` command (`hook-upload.ts:19-104`)

This appears to be a legacy/alternative hook path. It:
1. Reads from a `GazedConfig` at `~/.config/gazed/config.json` instead of `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`config.ts:5-6`, `hook-upload.ts:26-31`)
2. Uses the `readStdin()` and `parseStdinInput()` helpers from `stdin.ts` which handle TTY detection and Windows compatibility (`stdin.ts:14-40`)
3. Runs classification in parallel with git info resolution (`hook-upload.ts:56-59`)
4. Uses `GAZED_INGEST_ENDPOINT` env var, then `config.endpoint`, then `DEFAULT_ENDPOINT` (`hook-upload.ts:72-73`)
5. Does **not** resolve organization ID or include `gitRemote`/`packageName`

---

#### 6. Transcript Reading and Retry Logic (`transcript-reader.ts`)

**`readTranscript()`** (`transcript-reader.ts:4-31`): The **only** retry logic in the entire upload pipeline. It retries up to 5 times with 500ms delays. On each attempt:
- Checks if the file exists via `Bun.file().exists()`
- If the file doesn't exist, waits and retries (unless it's the last attempt, in which case throws)
- If reading throws any error, waits and retries (unless last attempt)

This retry is designed for timing issues where the transcript file may not be fully written yet (relevant for hook-triggered uploads where Claude Code is still finishing up).

---

### Data Flow

#### Interactive Upload Flow
1. `runUpload()` at `upload.ts:267` -- dispatches to interactive mode (no args)
2. `loadCredentials()` at `credentials.ts:35` -- reads `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
3. `scanProjects()` at `project-scanner.ts:28` -- reads `~/.claude/projects/` directories
4. `groupProjectsByRemote()` at `project-scanner.ts:110` -- groups by git remote with caching
5. User selects projects via `@clack/prompts` multiselect
6. `batchUpload()` at `batch-uploader.ts:82` -- sequential iteration
7. For each session: `uploadOneSession()` at `batch-uploader.ts:24`
   - `readTranscript()` at `transcript-reader.ts:4` (with 5x retry)
   - `extractAgentIds()` at `transcript-reader.ts:37`
   - `readSubagentFiles()` at `subagent-reader.ts:10`
   - `getGitInfo()` at `git-info.ts:25`
   - Optionally `classifySession()` at `classifier.ts:21`
   - `getProjectOrgId()` at `project-config.ts:68`
   - `uploadSession()` at `uploader.ts:15` -- oRPC call to server

#### Enable Flow
1. `runEnable()` at `enable.ts:15`
2. `verifyAuth()` at `auth.ts:22` -- loads credentials + API ping
3. `client.listMyOrganizations()` via `api-client.ts:11`
4. User selects org (or auto-select if single)
5. `setProjectOrgId()` at `project-config.ts:76` -- persists to `~/.rudel/projects.json`
6. `agent.installHook()` at `claude-code.ts:22` --> `addHook()` at `claude-settings.ts:77` -- writes to `.claude/settings.json`
7. `findSessionsForCwd()` at `project-scanner.ts:239` -- scans + groups + finds cwd group
8. User confirms bulk upload
9. `p.tasks()` iterates each session: read transcript, resolve git, upload

#### Hook Flow (Automatic)
1. Claude Code session ends, triggers `rudel hooks claude session-end`
2. `runSessionEnd()` at `session-end.ts:29`
3. Reads JSON from stdin (session_id, transcript_path, cwd)
4. `loadCredentials()` -- checks auth
5. `readTranscript()` -- with 5x retry
6. `extractAgentIds()` + `readSubagentFiles()`
7. `getGitInfo(cwd)` + `getProjectOrgId(cwd)`
8. `uploadSession()` -- single oRPC call

---

### Key Patterns

- **Agent Abstraction**: `CodingAgent` interface at `agents/types.ts:7-15` allows plugging in different coding tools. Currently only `ClaudeCodeAgent` is implemented (`agents/claude-code.ts:19`). `getDefaultAgent()` at `agents/index.ts:7` hardcodes `ClaudeCodeAgent`.
- **Remote Cache**: `remote-cache.ts` persists a `Record<string, string>` mapping encoded directory names to normalized git remote URLs at `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json`. Written fire-and-forget after grouping (`project-scanner.ts:218-220`).
- **Path Encoding/Decoding**: Claude Code encodes project paths by replacing `/` with `-`. Decoding uses recursive backtracking with filesystem `stat()` calls to handle ambiguous segments containing dashes (`session-resolver.ts:92-138`).
- **Two Credential Systems**: The newer path uses `~/.rudel/credentials.json` (`credentials.ts`), while `hook-upload.ts` reads a legacy config from `~/.config/gazed/config.json` (`config.ts:5-6`).

### Error Handling

| Location | Error Type | Handling |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `upload.ts:34-39` | Missing credentials | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:171-179` | Session resolution failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:184-192` | Transcript read failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1`, returns |
| `upload.ts:259-264` | Upload failure | Prints error from `UploadResult`, sets `process.exitCode = 1` |
| `batch-uploader.ts:33-39` | Transcript read failure in batch | Returns `{ success: false, error }`, counted in `result.failed` |
| `batch-uploader.ts:75-79` | Upload failure in batch | Returns `{ success: false, error }`, counted in `result.failed` |
| `uploader.ts:31-33` | Any oRPC error | Caught, returns `{ success: false, error: String(error) }` |
| `enable.ts:33-38` | Org fetch failure | Prints error, sets `process.exitCode = 1` |
| `enable.ts:156-159` | Session upload error in enable | Increments `failed` counter, returns error string as task result |
| `session-end.ts:80-81` | Any error in hook | Logged to file, never shown to user |
| `auth.ts:36-59` | Token verification | 401/403 clears stored credentials; other errors return `network_error` |

### Retry Logic

The **only** retry logic exists in `readTranscript()` at `transcript-reader.ts:4-31`:
- **Max retries**: 5
- **Delay**: 500ms between attempts
- **Scope**: File existence check and file read
- **Purpose**: Handle timing issues where transcript file is not yet fully written

There is **no retry logic** for:
- The oRPC upload call (`uploader.ts`)
- Git info resolution (`git-info.ts`)
- Subagent file reading (`subagent-reader.ts`)
- Classification (`classifier.ts`)

### Progress Reporting

| Context | Mechanism | Details |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Interactive upload | `@clack/prompts` spinner | Shows `[current/total] Uploading...` via callback at `upload.ts:116-118` |
| Interactive upload | Post-batch summary | Success count at `upload.ts:123-125`, failure count + first 5 errors at `upload.ts:126-134` |
| Single upload | `process.stdout.write` | Step-by-step text output (resolving, reading, transcript size, repo, branch, uploading, result) |
| Enable bulk upload | `p.tasks()` | Each task shows title `[i/total] sessionId`, updates message through "Reading transcript...", "Resolving git info...", "Uploading..." at `enable.ts:117-161` |
| Hook upload | File logging only | All output goes to `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` via `@logtape/logtape` at `logging.ts:7` |

### Configuration Files

| File | Purpose | Written by |
|------|---------|------------|
| `~/.rudel/credentials.json` | Auth token + API base URL | `credentials.ts:24-33` via `saveCredentials()` |
| `~/.rudel/projects.json` | Project-to-org mapping (keyed by normalized git remote or path) | `project-config.ts:29-37` via `setProjectOrgId()` |
| `~/.rudel/remote-cache.json` | Encoded-dir-to-remote mapping | `remote-cache.ts:33-42` via `cacheRemotes()` |
| `~/.rudel/logs/hook-upload.log` | Hook upload logs | `logging.ts:7` |
| `~/.config/gazed/config.json` | Legacy API key + endpoint config | `config.ts:5` (read by `hook-upload.ts`) |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Claude Code hook configuration | `claude-settings.ts:62-66` via `writeClaudeSettings()` |

### Command Flags (`upload`)

Defined at `upload.ts:277-329`:

| Flag | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `--tag` / `-t` | Enum (SESSION_TAGS) | None | Manual session tag |
| `--endpoint` | String | `https://app.rudel.ai/rpc` | Override upload URL |
| `--classify` / `-c` | Boolean | `false` | Auto-classify via Claude CLI |
| `--dry-run` / `-n` | Boolean | `false` | Preview without uploading |
| `--org` / `-o` | String | None | Override organization ID |

Now I understand the error structure. `ORPCError` extends `Error` and has:
- `code` - string like "BAD_GATEWAY"
- `status` - number like 502
- `message` - string like "Bad Gateway"
- `data` - any additional data

And there's an `instanceof` check via `Symbol.hasInstance` that should work even across contexts.

Now let me plan the implementation:

### Plan

1. **`uploader.ts`** - Add retry with exponential backoff for transient HTTP errors:
   - Import `ORPCError` from `@orpc/client`
   - Check `error instanceof ORPCError` and use `error.status` to determine retryability
   - Retry on 502, 503, 429 with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s)
   - Extract meaningful error info: `${error.code} (${error.status})` for ORPCErrors
   - Add `onRetry` callback to `UploadConfig` so callers can show retry status
   - Update `UploadResult` to include `attempts` count

2. **`enable.ts`** - Improve progress and summary:
   - Add succeeded counter alongside failed counts
   - Print proper summary: "X uploaded, Y failed"
   - The task return strings should include more context

3. **`batch-uploader.ts`** - Pass retry callback through

4. **`types.ts`** - Add `attempts` to `UploadResult`

I'm ready to start implementing. The plan is solid—I'll focus on retrying only transient errors (502, 503, 429) plus network failures, with a max of 3 attempts and exponential backoff of 1s then 2s. I'll also make sure to track attempts and pass the retry callback through the upload flow so callers can monitor progress.

Implementing now...

Now I have the full picture. Let me implement the improvements.

**Summary of issues:**
1. No retry on transient errors (502 Bad Gateway, 503, 429)
2. Error messages are opaque ("Error: Bad Gateway")
3. No progress counter visible after task completes in `enable` flow
4. No summary (succeeded/failed counts) at end
5. No way to debug failures

**Plan:**
1. Add retry with exponential backoff in `uploadSession()` for transient errors
2. Extract better error info from `ORPCError` (status code + message)
3. Improve `enable.ts` summary with succeeded/failed counts
4. Add `onRetry` callback so callers can show retry status

content.text: All 11 tasks pass (lint, type checks, tests, build). Let me review the final state of all changed files.

Everything looks good. Here's a summary of the changes:

### Changes made

**`apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts`** — Retry with exponential backoff
- Added retry logic: up to 3 attempts with 1s/2s backoff
- Retries on transient errors: 502 (Bad Gateway), 503 (Service Unavailable), 429 (Too Many Requests), and network `TypeError`s
- Better error formatting: `ORPCError` now shows `"502 Bad Gateway"` instead of `"Error: Bad Gateway"`
- Added `onRetry` callback to `UploadConfig` so callers can display retry status
- Returns `attempts` count in `UploadResult`

**`apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts`** — Added `attempts` field to `UploadResult`

**`apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts`** — Better progress and summary
- Added `succeeded` counter and `errors` array to track individual failures
- Summary now shows both success and failure counts: `"X session(s) uploaded"` / `"Y session(s) failed"`
- Lists up to 5 failed session IDs with their errors (same pattern as interactive upload)
- Shows retry status in the spinner: `"[3/117] Retrying (1/3) after 502 Bad Gateway..."`
- Shows `"Uploaded (after 2 attempts)"` when retries succeeded

**`apps/cli/src/lib/batch-uploader.ts`** — Retry visibility in interactive upload
- Added optional `status` parameter to `onProgress` callback
- Passes `onRetry` through to `uploadSession` so the spinner shows retry status

**`apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts`** — Updated spinner to show retry status

**`apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts`** — Better hook logging
- Logs retry attempts with session ID, attempt number, and error
- Logs success with attempt count, or failure with error message

content: also i think we need to track failed uploads, (after retries) in a file, so the user can easier, "later' try to reupload the failed ones.

message.content: also i think we need to track failed uploads, (after retries) in a file, so the user can easier, "later' try to reupload the failed ones.

content.text:
Good idea. Let me think about the right approach — store failed session paths so the user can retry them later with something like `rudel upload --retry`.

Let me first check how credentials/config files are already managed to follow the same pattern.

content.thinking:
Good. I see the pattern: all config files go in `~/.rudel/` (or `RUDEL_CONFIG_DIR`), using `getConfigDir()`. I need to:

1. Create a `failed-uploads.ts` module that persists failed uploads to `~/.rudel/failed-uploads.json`
2. Each entry should have: `sessionId`, `transcriptPath`, `projectPath`, `error`, `timestamp`, and optionally `organizationId`
3. After retries exhaust, record the failure
4. Add a `--retry` flag to the `upload` command that reads from this file and retries


5. On successful upload (including retry), remove from the file

Now I'm thinking through the file structure — it'll store an array of failure objects with sessionId, transcript and project paths, the error message, a timestamp, and optional organization ID.

For the implementation, I need to create a helper module with functions to read, write, and clear entries from this file, then wire it into the upload flow so failures get recorded after all retries are exhausted, successes get cleaned up from the file, and the `--retry` flag triggers a re-attempt of everything stored there. I need to implement the CRUD operations for the failed uploads file, then wire the recording into the relevant commands and add retry logic. For the `--retry` flow, I'm thinking it should read the failed uploads, show the user what needs retrying, get confirmation, then process each one and clean up successes from the file. This becomes a third mode alongside the existing interactive and single-session upload flows. Let me look more closely at how `uploadOneSession` works to understand what parameters I need to pass through.

For the retry mechanism, I'll store just the essential info: `sessionId`, `transcriptPath` (to derive `sessionDir`), `projectPath`, `organizationId`, plus some metadata like the error and timestamp. I'll create helper functions to read and append entries to the file while avoiding duplicates, and another to remove entries by `sessionId` once they succeed. Let me start building this out.

content.text: Good idea. I'll create a `failed-uploads.json` file in `~/.rudel/` to track failures, then add `rudel upload --retry` to retry them.
Now I have the patterns. Let me create the failed uploads module and wire everything together.

content.text: Now let's verify everything compiles and passes.

All 11 tasks pass, 34 tests pass. Here's the summary of the additional changes:

### New: Failed upload tracking + `--retry`

**`apps/cli/src/lib/failed-uploads.ts`** (new file)
- Persists failed uploads to `~/.rudel/failed-uploads.json`
- `recordFailedUpload()` — upserts by sessionId (stores transcriptPath, projectPath, organizationId, error, timestamp)
- `removeFailedUpload()` — removes on successful upload (including retries of previously failed sessions)
- `loadFailedUploads()` — reads the file for `--retry`

**`apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts`** — Added `--retry` / `-r` flag
- `rudel upload --retry` reads `~/.rudel/failed-uploads.json`, shows the failures with their errors and timestamps, confirms with the user, then retries each one
- Successful retries are removed from the file; still-failing ones are updated with the new error
- Shows succeeded/failed summary

**Wired into all upload paths:**
- **`enable.ts`** — Records failures, removes on success, shows "Run `rudel upload --retry`" hint
- **`batch-uploader.ts`** — Records failures, removes on success (covers interactive `rudel upload`)
- **`session-end.ts`** — Records failures from the async hook (so silent hook failures become visible via `--retry`)
- **`upload.ts`** interactive — Shows "Run `rudel upload --retry`" hint on failures

content.text:
All clean — 11 tasks pass, 35 tests pass, no lint warnings.

All features have been successfully re-applied on top of the refactored main. The changes adapted to the new architecture:

- **`@rudel/agent-adapters`** — the new adapter abstraction (`buildUploadRequest`, `findProjectSessions`, `source` field) is used everywhere instead of the old inline transcript reading/subagent extraction
- **Multi-agent support** — `enable.ts` loops over adapters (Claude Code + Codex), and the retry system uses `source` to pick the right adapter
- **Codex hook** — also gets failed upload tracking (new file from the refactor)
- **`batch-uploader.ts`** — no longer exists; the batch logic is inlined in `upload.ts`, and our retry/tracking is wired in there

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/santo-domingo/.claude/skills/pr-creation

# PR Creation Checklist

Follow these steps in order when creating a pull request. Do not skip any step.

## 1. Run Verification

```bash
bun run verify
```

This runs type checking, linting, and tests across the monorepo.

**Do NOT proceed if verification fails.** Fix issues first, then re-run.

## 2. Review the Workspace Diff

Use `mcp__conductor__GetWorkspaceDiff` with `stat: true` to see all changed files, then review the full diff to understand the scope of changes.

## 3. Commit All Changes

Stage and commit all changes. Follow any user instructions about commit message style.

## 4. Push and Create PR

1. Push to origin with `-u` flag if the branch has no upstream
2. PR title **must** use conventional commit format (enforced by CI):
   `feat:` | `fix:` | `docs:` | `style:` | `refactor:` | `perf:` | `test:` | `build:` | `ci:` | `chore:` | `revert:`
3. Use `gh pr create --base main` with:
   - Title under 80 characters
   - Description covering ALL changes in the workspace diff (not just the latest commit)
   - Keep description concise

```bash
gh pr create --base main --title "title here" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- bullet points describing changes

## Test plan
- [ ] testing steps

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Skipping verification**: Step 1 is non-negotiable. CI will catch the same failures but slower.
- **Wrong PR title format**: Must be conventional commit format or CI will reject it.
- **Describing only the latest commit**: The PR description should cover the entire branch diff.

Perfect! ✅ **PR created successfully:**

## PR Summary

**Title:** `feat: add retry and failed upload tracking to CLI uploads`

**Link:** https://github.com/obsessiondb/rudel/pull/96

**Key Features:**
- **Automatic retries** with exponential backoff on transient errors (502, 503, 429, network failures)
- **Progress visibility** during retries in interactive spinners
- **Failed upload tracking** persists to `~/.rudel/failed-uploads.json` for easy retry with `rudel upload --retry`
- **Better error reporting** with HTTP status codes + messages
- **Improved summaries** listing session IDs, errors, and timestamps with hints to retry

**Coverage:**
- ✅ All 11 turbo tasks pass (lint, type-check, test, build)
- ✅ All 35 tests pass
- ✅ Works on refactored main with multi-agent support (Claude Code + Codex)
- ✅ All upload paths: interactive, single-session, `enable` bulk, and async hooks
